mission.title: flux: Cannot seem to get the completions working in zsh
mission.description: Cannot seem to get the completions working in zsh. This issue affects 7+ users.

steps.title: Identify flux troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your flux deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review flux configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flux configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux
kubectl get configmap -n flux -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flux
```
### Describe the bug

Running the `. <(flux completion zsh)` command in the terminal or adding it to the ~/.zshrc file produce no completion results. changing the `.` to `source` also produce no results.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Cannot seem to get the completions working in zsh
steps.description:
This PR fixes `flux completion zsh` to work with `source` only.

`flux completion zsh` does not work with `source <(flux completion zsh)`, and needs to be executed as follows:

```
source <(flux completion zsh) && compdef _flux flux
```

This is confusing to Kubernetes users because `kubectl
```yaml

Fixes https://github.com/fluxcd/flux2/issues/971
Same here :-(
```

steps.title: Confirm Cannot seem to get the completions working in zsh is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flux -n flux --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n flux --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This PR fixes `flux completion zsh` to work with `source` only.

`flux completion zsh` does not work with `source <(flux completion zsh)`, and needs to be executed as follows:

```
source <(flux completion zsh) && compdef _flux flux
```

This is confusing to Kubernetes users because `kubectl completion zsh` works well with `source <(kubectl completion zsh)`.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flux installed or the issue environment reproducible.
